choices.text: Initializing and starting the Angular application

choices.text: Automatic synchronization of data between template and class

Property binding uses {{ }}, event binding uses []

choices.text: It contains project dependencies and scripts

choices.text: Ahead Of Time - compilation before loading
After Only Time - compilation after loading

questionList.question: Which command creates a new Angular application?

choices.text: It configures the CLI and Angular project settings

choices.text: A process that detects data changes and updates the view

A component that can only be used once

choices.text: Just In Time - compilation at runtime in the browser
Java In Time - compilation for Java

questionList.question: What is the main difference between AOT and JIT?

choices.text: AOT compiles before execution, JIT compiles during execution
JIT is used for production only

questionList.question: What is interpolation in Angular?

choices.text: Displaying dynamic values in the template with {{ }}

questionList.question: What is the role of the node_modules folder?

choices.text: It contains the project's npm dependencies

choices.text: The major version number of Angular

choices.text: By reloading the page on every navigation
Through SPA, lazy loading, and optimized change detection

By adding it to the module imports

choices.text: The path to the HTML template file

choices.text: A component with lots of styles
A component that manages business logic and data

choices.text: A component that only displays data and emits events

choices.text: The component updates only when @Input references change

choices.text: Injecting content from the parent component with <ng-content>

choices.text: By using ng-content with the select attribute

questionList.question: What is ng-template?

choices.text: An Angular element for defining reusable template fragments

questionList.question: What is ng-container?

choices.text: A container that creates a div in the DOM
A logical container that doesn't create an element in the DOM

questionList.question: What is *ngIf?

choices.text: A structural directive to conditionally show/hide an element

questionList.question: What is the difference between *ngIf and [hidden]?

choices.text: ngIf removes the element from DOM, hidden only hides it
hidden removes the element, ngIf only hides it

The last component on the page

choices.text: A set of directives to conditionally display elements

What is a Signal in Angular?

choices.text: A reactive wrapper around a value that notifies consumers of changes

questionList.question: Since which Angular version are Signals available?
How do you create a Signal with an initial value?
How do you update a Signal's value?
What is the difference between set() and update() on a Signal?

choices.text: set replaces the value, update calculates a new value from the old one
update replaces the value, set calculates a new value
set is for objects, update for primitives

questionList.question: What is a computed Signal?

choices.text: A Signal that automatically calculates its value from other Signals
A Signal optimized for math calculations
A Signal that can only be read once

questionList.question: How do you create a computed Signal?

choices.text: A function that automatically runs when dependent Signals change

questionList.question: How do you create an effect?
When does a computed Signal recalculate its value?

choices.text: Automatically when a dependent Signal changes

questionList.question: What is dependency injection with Signals?
What is a required Signal?

choices.text: A Signal that must be used
A way to declare a required @Input as a Signal
A Signal that cannot be null

How do you declare a Signal model for two-way binding?

choices.text: Signals are synchronous and simpler for state values

questionList.question: Can Signals coexist with RxJS?

choices.text: No, you must choose one or the other
Yes, toSignal() and toObservable() allow interoperability

questionList.question: What is toSignal()?

choices.text: Converts an Observable to a Signal
Converts a Signal to an Observable
Creates a Signal from a Promise
Creates a Signal from an Event

questionList.question: What is toObservable()?

choices.text: Converts a Signal to an Observable
Converts an Observable to a Signal
Creates an Observable from a Promise
Creates a Signal from an Event

choices.text: Signals can only be used in components
A Signal can be created in a service and shared between components

choices.text: Angular automatically knows when a Signal changes and updates the view

choices.text: A function to inject services returning Signals

choices.text: Yes, they only recalculate if dependencies changed
No, they recalculate on every call

choices.text: It's automatically destroyed when the component is destroyed

By setting the effect to null

choices.text: A Signal for managing outgoing events from a component

choices.text: TypeScript is Angular's primary language, bringing static typing

choices.text: A type combining multiple types with the | operator
A type that merges two interfaces

choices.text: type cannot be extended, interface can
type is more flexible (unions, intersections), interface is for objects

choices.text: A type that can only be used once
A parameterizable type that works with different types

questionList.question: How do you declare a generic?

choices.text: function fn(arg: T): T where T: generic

questionList.question: What is the any type in TypeScript?

choices.text: A type for any value, disabling type checking

questionList.question: What is the difference between any and unknown?

choices.text: unknown requires type checking before use, any doesn't

choices.text: An array with fixed types for each position

questionList.question: How do you declare a tuple?

choices.text: An enumerated type with a set of named constants

questionList.question: How do you declare an enum?

choices.text: A keyword to make a property immutable

choices.text: null is an intentionally absent value, undefined is an unassigned value

questionList.question: How do you make an interface property optional?
What is the never type in TypeScript?

choices.text: A type for values that will never occur

questionList.question: What is the void type in TypeScript?

choices.text: A type for functions that return nothing

questionList.question: How do you type a function in TypeScript?

choices.text: Both first and third are possible

questionList.question: What is the Pick type?

choices.text: A type to select certain properties from a type

questionList.question: What is the Omit type?

choices.text: A type to remove certain properties from a type

questionList.question: What is the Partial type?

choices.text: Makes all properties of a type optional

questionList.question: What is the Required type?
What is the Record type?

choices.text: A type to create an object with typed keys and values

questionList.question: How do you type rest parameters in a function?

choices.text: TypeScript automatically deduces the type of a variable

questionList.question: What is the ReturnType type?

choices.text: A type to extract a function's return type
A type to define a return type

questionList.question: What is a service in Angular?

choices.text: A class for managing business logic and data sharing

choices.text: A design pattern where Angular provides dependencies to classes

choices.text: By creating an instance with new
By declaring it in the constructor

choices.text: The service is only available in the root component

choices.text: providedIn is more modern and allows tree-shaking

questionList.question: What is a singleton service?

choices.text: A service with a single shared instance in the application

By declaring it in a component's providers

questionList.question: What is a Subject in RxJS?

choices.text: Subject can emit values with next(), Observable cannot

choices.text: Takes values until another Observable emits

choices.text: Retries an Observable on error based on a condition

choices.text: inject() can be used outside the constructor

choices.text: A container for grouping components, directives, pipes, and services

questionList.question: Which decorator is used to create an NgModule?

Which property imports other modules?

choices.text: The main module bootstrapped in the application (usually AppModule)

questionList.question: Which @NgModule property indicates the root component?

choices.text: A module that groups a specific application feature

questionList.question: What is a Shared Module?

choices.text: A module shared between multiple apps

questionList.question: What is a Core Module?

choices.text: A module for singleton services and single-use components

questionList.question: Why is using a Core Module with providedIn: 'root' often redundant?

choices.text: Services cannot be in Core Modules

questionList.question: What is BrowserModule?

choices.text: The module required for browser applications

questionList.question: What is CommonModule?

questionList.question: Why is HttpClientModule obsolete?

questionList.question: What is moduleId in a component?

choices.text: A reference to the component's module for relative paths (deprecated)

questionList.question: Why are standalone components preferred over NgModules?

choices.text: Less boilerplate, clearer structure, better tree-shakability
